title: equisetum arvense subsp. diffusum
intro: Equisetum arvense subsp. diffusum, also known as field horsetail or marsh horsetail, is a subspecies of the Equisetum arvense plant, a type of vascular plant that has been used medicinally for centuries. The plant is rich in silica, which is believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic effects. Equisetum arvense subsp. diffusum has been used to treat a range of health issues, including kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions, due to its ability to promote the elimination of toxins and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of field horsetail typically involve drying and processing the plant's stems, which are then used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
